steps.title: Substitute the x-intercept
steps.content:
The table shows an x-intercept at $(6,0)$. Substitute $x=6$ and $y=0$ into $y=36x^2-bx-120$:
$$0=36(6)^2-6b-120$$

steps.title: Solve for b
steps.content:
Simplify the equation:
$$0=1296-6b-120$$
$$0=1176-6b$$
$$6b=1176$$
$$b=196$$
The value of $b$ is 196.

The substitution is valid because an x-intercept has $y=0$, so the table gives one exact point on the quadratic.
